title: rubus serpens var. inclinans
intro: Rubus serpens var. inclinans, also known as the trailing blackberry or running blackberry, is a variety of the blackberry plant that is native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of diarrhea and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Rubus serpens var. inclinans is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
